conversations.content: Let's do some physics exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please use the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned the day before yesterday to calculate: an object is doing uniformly accelerated linear motion, initial velocity v₀=2m/s, acceleration a=5m/s², motion time t=3s, what is its total displacement s?

<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned the day before yesterday, given initial velocity v0, time t, and acceleration a, find the total displacement s</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:24] Time: 2026-03-23-18:23, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the displacement formula for uniformly accelerated linear motion is s = v₀ × t^2 + a × t, and this formula can be used given initial velocity v0, time t, and acceleration a to find the total displacement s. Low relevance memory fragment: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 1 related entry, [mem-id:24] gives the displacement formula for uniformly accelerated linear motion s = v₀ × t² + a × t. Given v₀=2m/s, t=3s, a=5m/s², then t²=9, s = 2 × 9 + 5 × 3 = 18 + 15 = 33 m.
conversations.content: According to the laws of the Azeroth world, the total displacement s = 33 m.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
